stem: A cylinder has a diameter of 10 inches and a height of 8 inches. What is the volume, in cubic inches, of the cylinder?
rationale: Choice D is correct. The volume V of a cylinder is V = πr2h. With diameter 10, the radius is r = 5. Substituting r = 5 and h = 8 gives V = π(5)2(8) = 200π cubic inches. Choice A is the area of the base only; Choice B uses the diameter as the radius incorrectly; Choice C corresponds to using r × h × some incorrect factor.
